_meta.purpose: Complete lossless handoff of the STRIKERS research landing page design system, architecture, components, and functions. Load into a new Claude chat to build or adapt a new research landing page for a different study under the same STRIKERS design system.
_meta.opening_instruction: I am building a new STRIKERS Elite Sports Group research landing page. This JSON contains the complete design system, component architecture, and function specifications from the Performance Medicine landing page. Adapt all content for [NEW STUDY NAME] while keeping the design system, layout patterns, and functional components identical. Read the entire JSON before starting.

project_context.organisation: STRIKERS Elite Sports Group, Dublin, Ireland
project_context.co_institution: University of St Andrews, St Andrews, UK
project_context.note: Each new study gets its own GitHub repo, GA Property, and GA Stream. The GA account is shared.

primary_logo.description: Combined horizontal logo — St Andrews crest left, vertical grey divider, strikerselite sports injury group dublin right
primary_logo.use: Nav (left side) and footer (centred) — single asset replaces separate logos
primary_logo.background: White or light only — logo has no padding, place on white/pale backgrounds

partner_banner.description: Full-width horizontal strip of collaborative institution logos including Cricket Scotland, DP World Tour, Sunshine Tour, Aspetar Mediclinic, Fortius Clinic, Sport and Exercise Scotland, Commonwealth Games Glasgow 2026, International Olympic Committee, Scottish Network, STRIKERS Elite
partner_banner.placement: Between hero section and programme/papers section
partner_banner.label: Collaborative institutions and partners
partner_banner.style: White background, 1px border top and bottom, 36px logo height, horizontally scrollable on mobile
partner_banner.note: Reuse same banner for all STRIKERS pages — it represents the overarching group partnerships

qr_code_generation.note: Generate a new QR code for each study pointing to that study's landing page URL. The QR on the webpage itself should point to the survey URL.

ink.use: Primary text, nav links active, dark buttons

border.use: All card borders, dividers, table lines

pale.use: Section backgrounds, card hover, row headers

palette.note: The four quadrant colours and OA colour below are PMIF-specific. Adapt for other study frameworks.

ali.use: Allied Health and Scientific / PM2 tag / email button

oa.use: Overarching Aspects / PMIF arch-card / hero title accent / comparison table PM column

pm1_header.use: PM1 poster header / PM1 poster View button

pm2_header.use: PM2 poster header / PM2 poster View button

typography.body_use: Section titles (800), hero title (800), body prose (400), lead text (300)
typography.mono_use: ALL labels, eyebrows, buttons, tags, codes, abstract text, table content, captions, nav links, footer text, drawer headers

page_architecture.all_assets_embedded: All images base64 encoded inline — no external image requests
page_architecture.no_frameworks: Vanilla JS only — no React, Vue, jQuery etc

css_architecture.approach: CSS custom properties (variables) for palette, flat class naming
css_architecture.no_css_framework: No Tailwind, Bootstrap etc — pure custom CSS

nav.left_content: Combined STRIKERS + St Andrews logo (38px height)
nav.right_content: Nav links — JetBrains Mono 9px uppercase letter-spacing 0.06em
nav.nav_links: Anchor links to page sections — Papers | Compare | Domains | Tools | Survey (adapt for new study)
nav.mobile_behaviour: nav-left has min-width:0 and overflow:hidden to prevent cutoff on narrow screens

sections_template.purpose: First impression — study figure dominant, brief title and tagline
components: h1 title with accent colour on key word
domain annotation strips (2x2 or 4x1 on desktop)
sections_template.animations: All elements fadeUp sequential, figure scaleIn at 0.4s
sections_template.domain_strips: 2-col mobile / 4-col desktop grid of small cards with left border colour matching quadrant, JetBrains Mono 8px content

sections_template.purpose: Show institutional partnerships — trust signal between hero and content
sections_template.placement: Immediately after hero, before papers section
sections_template.reuse: Same banner image across all STRIKERS pages

components_per_paper: paper-card with left border colour (by paper type)
abstract body — all sections with abs-lbl + abs-txt in JetBrains Mono 10px 300 weight
poster-btns bar (View poster | Download poster | Share poster)
poster-drawer (inline image viewer, dark background, scrollable)
sections_template.poster_drawer_behaviour: toggleDrawer(id) — classList.toggle open, scrollIntoView on open

sections_template.purpose: Position the study field relative to adjacent disciplines
sections_template.note: Include if the study introduces or positions a new field. Omit for purely empirical studies.

table_design.study_column_style: background:#F0EEFB (light purple), color:var(--oa), font-weight:500 — visually dominant

sections_template.purpose: Show the study framework structure in detail
sections_template.note: Adapt quad-grid for the specific framework of the new study. If no quadrant structure, use a list or different grid.
sections_template.quad_panel_design: border-radius 3px, relative position for ghost number (38px 0.08 opacity), JetBrains Mono 8.5px uppercase h3, domain items 10.5px Inter

sections_template.purpose: Provide downloadable outputs and inline readers
sections_template.note: Include if the study produced tools, forms, or documents for download.

arch_card_pattern.structure: border-left 3px solid colour + card header (tag + h3) + card body (p + spec-list + tool-btns)
arch_card_pattern.download_buttons: dl-btn class — coloured background, white text, JetBrains Mono 9px
arch_card_pattern.inline_reader: view-btn opens os-drawer — dark header with title and close button, scrollable page images with lazy loading
arch_card_pattern.spec_list: dashed border-bottom rows, sp-dot 5px circle + JetBrains Mono 9px text

sections_template.purpose: Primary conversion — survey participation + secondary sharing and contact
components: QR code block (pale background, border, 148px QR image, JetBrains Mono label)
Primary CTA: Take the survey → (s-btn-primary, ink background, full width max 280px)
Secondary group: Share survey | Share page | Email | LinkedIn (s-btn-sm, outline style, flex-wrap row)

share_api.mobile: navigator.share() — triggers native iOS/Android share sheet
share_api.confirmation: share-confirm div — show class for 3s then remove
share_api.email_button: mailto: link with pre-populated subject line
share_api.linkedin_button: target=_blank link to CI LinkedIn profile

sections_template.qr_points_to: The landing page URL (not the survey URL — survey URL is on the page itself)

scroll_reveal.apply_to: All section eyebrows, titles, leads, cards, tables

drawer_toggle.behaviour: classList.toggle('open') on drawer element, scrollIntoView smooth on open

drawer_structure.header: div.drawer-top — ink background, flex between title and close button

share_functions.doShare_logic: if navigator.share → native share sheet; else navigator.clipboard.writeText → show shareDone confirmation for 3s
share_functions.PAGE_URL: Hardcoded to live GitHub Pages URL — not window.location.href
share_functions.SURVEY_URL: Hardcoded to MS Forms or equivalent survey URL

interactive_components.nav_scroll: html { scroll-behavior: smooth } — all anchor links scroll smoothly

poster_buttons.view: toggleDrawer — opens inline image viewer below card
poster_buttons.download: HTML anchor with download attribute pointing to PDF filename in same repo
poster_buttons.share: sharePoster() — native share on mobile, clipboard fallback

scroll_cue.name: fadeIn
scroll_cue.structure: flex column, scroll-line (1px gradient) + 'scroll' text in JetBrains Mono 8px light

github_deployment.pattern: One repo per study under andrewhallorthosports
github_deployment.pages_setup: POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pages with source branch main — repo must be public on free plan

large_file_push_method.why: Files >1MB cannot be pushed via simple GitHub Contents API
large_file_push_method.method: Git blob API — create blob, create tree, create commit, update ref

google_analytics.per_study: New Property + new Web Stream per study
google_analytics.snippet:
<!-- Google Analytics GA4 -->
<script async src='https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?id=G-XXXXXXXXXX'></script>
<script>
  window.dataLayer = window.dataLayer || [];
  function gtag(){dataLayer.push(arguments);}
  gtag('js', new Date());
  gtag('config', 'G-XXXXXXXXXX');
</script>

reusable_assets_for_all_strikers_pages.github_token: Stored with Andrew — new token if expired, same scope (repo classic PAT)
standing_rules: No em dashes — use colons, commas, or parentheses
No AI or synthesis caveats within figures or on-page content
Conservative language — explicit uncertainty where it exists
Structural decisions are Andrew's — Claude executes

before_building: Define study title (both papers if applicable)
Have survey URL (MS Forms or equivalent)
Have any downloadable tool files (PDFs, DOCXs)
content_to_replace: All study titles and paper tags
Domain strips content (quadrant names and domain lists)
Comparison table columns and rows (if applicable)
Survey URL and QR code image (base64)
to_keep_identical: All JS functions (toggleDrawer, doShare, shareSurvey, sharePage, sharePoster, IntersectionObserver)
All button patterns and drawer patterns

smec_next_page.note: This is Andrew's prior analogous work — the visual and structural template for PM. A new landing page for SMEC should follow this same design system exactly.
smec_next_page.survey_note: SMEC survey URL and QR code details were in a prior chat session — retrieve from that session before building
